name: 细胞器
subject: biology
curriculum_points: 阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动
举例说明细胞各部分结构之间相互联系、协调一致，共同执行细胞的各项生命活动
建构并使用细胞模型，阐明细胞各部分结构通过分工与合作，形成相互协调的有机整体，实现细胞水平的各项生命活动
尝试制作真核细胞的结构模型

textbook_content.curriculum_standard: 普通高中课程标准（2017年版2020年修订）
textbook_content.subject: biology
textbook_content.textbook_alignment: 与统编生物学课程标准及课标内容要求一致

deep_snippets.text: Some cells contain aggregates of macromolecules surrounded by membranes; these are called organelles. Organelles are small structures that exist within cells. Examples of organelles include mitochondria and chloroplasts, which carry out indispensable functions: mitochondria produce energy to power the cell, while chloroplasts enable green plants to utilize the energy in sunlight to make sugars. All living things are made of cells; the cell itself is the smallest fundamental unit of structure and function in living organisms. (This requirement is why viruses are not considered living: they are not made of cells. To make new viruses, they have to invade and hijack the reproductive mechanism of a living cell; only then can they obtain the materials they need to reproduce.) Some organisms consist of a single cell and others are multicellular. Cells are classified as prokaryotic or eukaryotic. Prokaryotes are single-celled or colonial organisms that do not have membranebound nuclei; in contrast, the cells of eukaryotes do have membrane-bound organelles and a membrane-bound nucleus. In larger organisms, cells combine to make tissues, which are groups of similar cells carrying out similar or related functions. Organs are collections of tissues grouped together performing a common function. Organs are present not only in animals but also in plants. An organ system is a higher level of organization that consists of functionally related organs. Mammals have many organ systems. For instance, the circulatory system transports blood through the body and to and from the lungs; it include

(a) (b) Figure 4.8 These figures show the major organelles and other cell components of (a) a typical animal cell and (b) a typical eukaryotic plant cell. The plant cell has a cell wall, chloroplasts, plastids, and a central vacuole—structures not found in animal cells. Most plant cells do not have lysosomes or centrosomes. If the nucleolus were not able to carry out its function, what other cellular organelles would be affected? a. The structure of endoplasmic reticulum would not form. b. The function of lysosomes would be hindered, as hydrolases are formed by nucleolus. c. The free ribosomes and the rough endoplasmic reticulum, which contains ribosomes, would not form. Chapter 4 | Cell Structure
> **[Figure 132, Page 168]** A large illustration or diagram (519×587px) related to: Figure, figures, show, major, organelles, other. This image appears on page 168 of the textbook.
![Figure 132 - A large illustration or diagram (519×587px) related to: Figu](OpenStax_AP_Biology_images/img_0132_c8551e7f.png)
> **[Figure 133, Page 168]** A large illustration or diagram (544×459px) related to: Figure, figures, show, major, organelles, other. This image appears on page 168 of the textbook.
![Figure 133 - A large illustration or diagram (544×459px) related to: Figu](OpenStax_AP_Biology_images/img_0133_c8551e7f.png)

By the end of this section, you will be able to do the following: • Describe the structure of eukaryotic cells • Compare animal cells with plant cells • State the role of the plasma membrane • Summarize the functions of the major cell organelles Have you ever heard the phrase “form follows function?” It’s a philosophy that many industries follow. In architecture, this means that buildings should be constructed to support the activities that will be carried out inside them. For example, a skyscraper should include several elevator banks. A hospital should have its emergency room easily accessible. Our natural world also utilizes the principle of form following function, especially in cell biology, and this will become clear as we explore eukaryotic cells (Figure 4.8). Unlike prokaryotic cells, eukaryotic cells have: 1) a membranebound nucleus; 2) numerous membrane-bound organelles such as the endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, chloroplasts, mitochondria, and others; and 3) several, rod-shaped chromosomes. Because a membrane surrounds eukaryotic cell’s nucleus, it has a “true nucleus.” The word “organelle” means “little organ,” and, as we already mentioned, organelles have specialized cellular functions, just as your body's organs have specialized functions. At this point, it should be clear to you that eukaryotic cells have a more complex structure than prokaryotic cells. Organelles allow different functions to be compartmentalized in different areas of the cell. Before turning to organelles, let’s first examine two important components of the cell: the plasma membrane

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 细胞器

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物学
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 10
- **章节/领域**: 细胞的结构
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动
> 举例说明细胞各部分结构之间相互联系、协调一致，共同执行细胞的各项生命活动
> 建构并使用细胞模型，阐明细胞各部分结构通过分工与合作，形成相互协调的有机整体，实现细胞水平的各项生命活动
> 尝试制作真核细胞的结构模型

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「细胞器」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「细胞器」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「细胞器」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
2. 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「细胞器」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系健康、生态、农业或生物技术案例，讨论「细胞器」与现实生活的关系。
- 通过模型图、显微图或调查资料拓展学生对生命系统的理解。

errors.description: 把「细胞器」当作孤立事实记忆，不能说明结构、功能和生命过程之间的关系。
解释生命现象时缺少证据，容易把相关关系误认为因果关系。

exercises.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「细胞器」的现象解释或实验探究题。
exercises.answer: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。

exercises.stem: 用结构与功能或过程与结果的关系解释「细胞器」。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：阐明细胞内具有多个相对独立的结构，担负着物质运输、合成与分解、能量转换和信息传递等生命活动，说明生命现象、结构/过程和功能意义。

exercises.stem: 举一个生活、健康或生态案例，说明「细胞器」的应用价值。
exercises.answer: 需写出案例、涉及的生物学原理以及对生活或环境的意义。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「细胞器」相关的生命现象。
